Criteria: An immediate, severe threat to the public safety of an entire community or region due to large-scale rioting, violent civil disorder, or hostile actions. Citizens must seek immediate shelter or follow evacuation directives.

Active, localized security threats requiring direct police tactical intervention (e.g., active shooter, armed barricaded suspect, or localized violent crowd convergence). Citizens must avoid the area completely.
An legally enforceable emergency declaration restricting all public movement and pedestrian/vehicular traffic within defined geographic boundaries during specific hours to restore public order.
A broadcast warning regarding an ongoing atmospheric change or systemic disruption to public infrastructure/safety caused by human activity, protests, or institutional shutdowns.

A general emergency notification for conditions requiring immediate public awareness, but not yet escalating to a full regional warning (e.g., a localized civil disturbance impacting public transit hubs).

An official, legally binding announcement by incident command stating that a gathering has become violent or disorderly, revoking the right to assemble and ordering immediate dispersal under penalty of arrest.
A non-emergency advisory detailing large-scale, peaceful gatherings or marches causing extensive road closures, transit delays, or minor crowd friction zones.
